response.justification: Strong geospatial domain expertise and distributed systems background directly align with the routing pipeline domain. However, the critical gap is lack of modern C++ experience—last hands-on C++ work was over a decade ago at National Instruments, while the role requires commercial-grade modern C++ as a primary skill. The candidate shows strong learning ability across diverse tech stacks, which could support ramping up on C++, but this represents significant onboarding risk for a mid-senior individual contributor role.
strengths: Deep geospatial/GIS domain expertise from 8+ years building geo-data platforms and mapping applications, directly relevant to routing tiles
Proven experience building high-performance distributed systems handling millions of requests/second with async I/O optimization at Dunnhumby
Demonstrated ability to learn and master multiple programming languages across diverse technical domains (D, Node.js, Java, Swift, C#)
concerns: C++ experience is dated (2014-2015 at National Instruments) with no recent hands-on systems programming in modern C++, which is the primary technical requirement
Most recent 4 years of work focused on full-stack web development (TypeScript, Node.js, React) rather than systems-level infrastructure or production routing pipelines
No demonstrated experience with routing optimization, navigation data processing, or production routing infrastructure specifically

key_factors.factor: Distributed systems and performance optimization background
